name: Assessment Stakes and Use Boundaries Search Pass

search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: "synthetic responses" formative summative automated scoring validation
search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for targeted query
screened first result page for targeted query

search_queries.query: Standards for Educational and Psychological Testing intended use validity stakes official PDF
ETS AI assessment guidelines validity fairness transparency monitoring official PDF
search_queries.result_count: official ETS executive summary PDF verified

search_queries.query: U.S. Department of Education formative assessment feedback instruction official

inclusion_criteria: Direct synthetic-response evidence must involve synthetic, simulated, generated, corrupted, or analogous student, learner, essay, short-answer, constructed-response, chatbot-input, or open-response assessment data.

Direct evidence must connect synthetic responses to assessment purpose, decision consequence, or workflow role such as formative feedback, training augmentation, pre-pilot validation, stress testing, or scorer validation.
Contextual guidance may be included when it defines assessment-stakes, formative/summative, validity, fairness, or AI-assessment guardrails needed to interpret synthetic-response use boundaries.
Direct findings require full text, full-text HTML, official guidance, or previously verified full-text source records.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude generic automated scoring studies that do not include synthetic responses in the response corpus or development workflow.
Exclude generic synthetic data policy sources unless they directly inform educational assessment validity, stakes, formative feedback, or AI assessment controls.
Exclude sources that support only technical realism or model performance without a clear assessment-use boundary.
Do not treat contextual standards guidance as direct empirical evidence about synthetic-response performance.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, canonical source page, official PDF URL, and existing public graph source_id.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 20 records across targeted web, official guidance, and existing-graph screening

screening_decisions.title: Standards for Educational and Psychological Testing
screening_decisions.reason: Official professional standards provide the intended-use and stakes-sensitive validity frame needed to bound acceptable synthetic-response uses.

screening_decisions.title: Responsible Use of AI in Assessment
screening_decisions.reason: Official ETS guidance supplies AI-assessment guardrails around validity, fairness, transparency, human oversight, and monitoring.

screening_decisions.title: TEAL Center Fact Sheet No. 9: Formative Assessment
screening_decisions.reason: LINCS/TEAL practice guidance distinguishes formative feedback and instructional-support contexts from more consequential assessment uses.

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Live Scoring Constructed Response Items with Commercial LLMs
screening_decisions.reason: Previously verified full text directly distinguishes synthetic pre-pilot checks from real-response pilot validation in a constructed-response scoring workflow.

screening_decisions.title: DREsS: Dataset for Rubric-based Essay Scoring on EFL Writing
screening_decisions.reason: Previously verified full text directly supports a lower-consequence training-augmentation boundary where real essays carry validation and test evidence.

screening_decisions.title: Is Lunch Free Yet? Overcoming the Cold-Start Problem in Supervised Content Scoring using Zero-Shot LLM-Generated Training Data
screening_decisions.reason: Previously verified full text supplies a label-audit boundary for lower-consequence classroom scorer-development use.

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Designing an AI Chatbot to Support Scientific Argumentation
screening_decisions.reason: Previously verified full text provides lower-stakes formative feedback/prototype evidence using synthetic student inputs.

screening_decisions.title: Best of both worlds: combining LLMs and traditional ML for automated scoring of an open-response situational judgment test
screening_decisions.reason: Previously verified full text provides adjacent high-stakes admissions evidence for synthetic demographic analogs as fairness stress-test material.

screening_decisions.title: Evaluating LLMs for Automated Scoring in Formative Assessments
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to lower-stakes LLM automated scoring but does not use synthetic student responses as the scored or development corpus.

screening_decisions.title: A Multilingual Dataset of Student Answers, Human Grading, and Multi-LLM Evaluations for Automated Assessment Research Using JorGPT
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to automated assessment benchmarking but reports authentic student answers rather than synthetic student responses.

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging Knowledge Profiles and Generative AI for Realistic Student Response Generation
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially relevant to in-term synthetic response generation for automated grading, but direct full-text access was not established in this pass.

screening_summary: Targeted bootstrap screening combined general assessment-stakes guidance with direct synthetic-response evidence from the existing public graph. The resulting baseline treats standards and formative-assessment guidance as boundary context, and treats synthetic-response studies as direct evidence only where they actually connect response origin to purpose, consequence, and operational use.
limitations: This was a targeted bootstrap pass, not a systematic review.
Contextual guidance sources are not synthetic-response-specific and should not be used as direct evidence of synthetic-response scoring validity.
Direct high-stakes synthetic-response evidence remains sparse and mostly diagnostic.
The pass did not establish full-text access to all potentially relevant in-term or automated-grading generation studies.
